title: Level 62
wikitext:
{{CSS|content= .page__main, .page-content, #mw-content-text, .mw-parser-output, .page-header, .page-header__title, .page-header__actions, .page-footer, .page-footer__bottom, .page-footer__content, .page-footer__meta { background-color: black !important; color: white !important; } .right-rail-wrapper, .rail-module { background-color: black !important; color: white !important; } a { color: #b8b8b8; } }} {{Background|file=Reverseimagebg.jpg}} {{DISPLAYTITLE:‎}}
[[File:sheiswaiting3.png|center|frameless|2510x2510px|link=]]
"All I have to do is find the girl and get out of here," I told myself as I entered this place. I always considered myself empathetic. I was always eager to help those in need. Now was no different. There is a girl called Ash. She was reported missing on this level. Not much is known about the exact location where she was last seen, but looking at the whole thing with my own eyes, it became a bit obvious. This level was nothing but a single suburban home. I'll be honest, it didn't look quite nice. It looked ruined, in fact. Windows shattered, and I even saw some holes in the walls. And yet I noticed some rooms still had light shining through. I'm sure I could still fool someone into thinking somebody lives here. I looked around the surroundings and beyond the horizon. I saw a brick wall. I think it served its purpose as a barrier for the level. Which was weird, as when I stepped forward, the wall would get higher, and yet I could see street light lamps in the distance, far beyond the wall. But I knew I wouldn't be able to get up close. I went towards the house. The ground below me felt nicer than I expected, given the building. I took my time to get to the building. I wasn't in a rush, as nothing really seemed dangerous. Looking at the sky, I noticed it was twilight. I really liked that time of day. Before I got here, I used to go out every day just to look at it. I stopped for a second to look, but continued shortly after. I had a job to do, ever since I entered this place, I no longer been able to enjoy small things. It's too dangerous. But the sky made me look at other things, and walk a bit slower. I noticed a front and back yard. They were both gardens, but they weren't the nice-looking ones. Most of the plants have already died, and the weeds have taken over. Looks like this level required human interaction, but no one was quite eager to help. All of these conclusions quickly formed a shower thought. Was this place always ruined? Perhaps at some point, the building was in a perfect state, and the flowers bloomed in the sunlight. It seems I won't know for a long time, as I wasn't really told much about the level, because, as it turns out, nobody really knows much about it. That made me turn a blind eye. Otherwise, I would ask for more info. {{Underline|FFFFFF}} I entered the house. As it turns out, the inside is much more vast than its outside. However, that sighting wasn't so beautiful, as each corridor seemed incredibly messy, full of trash and mold. I knew I had to be quick; staying near these things can't be good in the long run. As I walked through, my mind started behaving more unusually. And a feeling of paranoia crept in. I began hearing creaking, breathing, and other weird sounds, but most importantly, I felt eyes on my back. Yet when I turned around, there was no one to be found. But I continued to walk slowly, and tried to get myself distracted by counting just how much of the trash was something I could actually name. It worked for a while, but after a long time, I felt the floor shaking under my feet. I stopped moving, but as it turns out, I wasn't the cause of the shaking. Suddenly, it got more aggressive, and I heard plates and cups breaking in other rooms. I tried to be competent in controlling everything, but by then, I was so lost in my thoughts I didn't even realize they were leading me to a bedroom. When I snapped out, I found myself right in front of an open door. {{Underline|FFFFFF}} Facing this room, I was a bit hesitant to enter. However, I thought if my thoughts led me there, then their thoughts should have also led her there. So I opened the door. Suddenly, the door behind me slammed shut. I immediately tried to get out, but the door wouldn't budge. It took me a long time to realize the shaking stopped. It seemed as if whoever ran this level was deliberately waiting for me to enter this room. I looked around for more ways to escape, but found nothing. There was no window, and there was no way I was going to break a wall with the tools I had. Hopeless, I looked around the room. There wasn't much: a bed and a closet. But the room didn't seem that clean either, I couldn't count the number of scratches on the walls even if I stayed here for a week. Someone was here before. The bed also didn't have a blanket or a pillow, only a white mattress. The closet was on the other side of the room compared to the bed, and was a regular closet. When I opened it, I saw a notebook. I opened it and went through the pages. One caught my attention. ''"I won't even mark this. I tried before, but I'm obviously wrong. How am I supposed to know the date? I tried sliding letters under the door for someone to find, but it seems there's no one here, so I stopped trying. I'm starting to feel deranged, as if something is here that I don't know of, and it's trying to take over me. I've noticed I have started to grow faster, and today I can reach the roof with my hand. When I got here, I couldn't even reach it when I stepped on the bed. Sometimes when I get tired, I start feeling this pain in some parts of my body. I don't know what's happening, but I hope I'll get help soon. I'm getting very worried. I also keep seeing that man. He was standing in the doorway of a bedroom when I came out. Now I know he’s real."'' Some words were pretty hard to make out because there was a bit of blood on the pages, but I managed to make them out. I tried to read the others, but was disturbed by a footstep right behind me. A sliver of fear went through my whole body as I turned around.
It was her.
{{Warning|content=jumpscares}}
[[File:sheiswaiting3.png|center|frameless|2510x2510px|link=]]

What was her name?
[[File:sheishere3.png|center|frameless|2510x2510px|link=]]
Ash.
The new resident has come. To reside forever.
As she stays, she will make sure to make things way better.
Take care of her, and she shall take care of trespassers.
She will make sure they will not become filthy seekers.
She will have fun in her new body as a monster.
It will help her; she will be known as the best stopper. Her height stretched out over the horizon and beyond,
She will grow up to be of that height, forever fond.
Her large black claws, that none will be able to escape,
Will be a fun time, like looking at the best landscape.
Her large mouth to catch and feed on the poor prey she catches.
No one will escape, no tool will help them, not wrenches. Man in the shadows, please promise me just this one thing.
The promise you'll always keep, beyond and in the spring.
You will take care of the girl, and you will lead the food.
It won't matter whether you are or not in the mood.
For I knew from the very start your true intentions,
To slaughter them all, no matter their great inventions.
She will take care of anything else, I promise you.
It may take a while, but she will; that is very true.
But don't let her escape, as she is our only hope.
Do anything if she tries, even pull with a rope,
She is perfect for this, unlike everyone else here,
You'll not find anyone who is ever like her near. Don't worry about anyone else; they won't matter.
They'll focus on something else at this level after.
Those who find out, she will not let them come out alive,
This place stays "secret" no matter who's gonna arrive.
You'll defend her; she shall be our brand new protector,
As I have never seen, even as the "director,"
This talent, before, I will say I am quite impressed,
And for her enthusiasm, it needs to be addressed. Dear Ash, it's time for you to prove the weight of your worth,
The Man from the shadow shall lead your far future growth.
Someone has entered, so it's time for him to be led,
By the man you saw before, you didn't know you fed.
So, Ash, it is time to get up, and you'll stop the blunt,
As for your first time of many, it is time to hunt. [[File:SleepKiller.jpeg|center|frameless|455x455px|link=]]
